dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6664 IH: To establish a Biotechnology Management Office in the Department of Defense and to provide support for the research and development of bioindustrial manufacturing processes, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Neguse introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To establish a Biotechnology Management Office in the Department of Defense and to provide support for the research and development of bioindustrial manufacturing processes, and for other purposes.

subsection.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense shall designate a senior official, with relevant biotechnology experience, from a position within the Department of Defense that was in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act, to—

paragraph.text: be the senior official for biotechnology issues;
be the head of the Biotechnology Management Office established under subsection (b); and
carry out the responsibilities for the office in subsection (c).

subsection.text: Not later than 120 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense shall, with input from the senior official designated under subsection (a), charter and establish, under the authority, direction, and control of the Deputy Secretary of Defense, a Biotechnology Management Office to foster the development, acquisition, and sustainment of broad-based biotechnology capabilities for the Department.
The office established under subsection (b) shall be responsible for the following:

paragraph.text: Maintaining and executing a long-term research, development, acquisition, and sustainment roadmap.
Updating policies and guidance within the Department relating to the acquisition, adoption, and transition of biotechnology-based products into Department use.
Coordinating with activities across the Department, the Federal Government, industry, academia, and international partners relating to biotechnology.
Proposing options for streamlining the regulatory or acquisition process of the Department.
Conducting, as may be needed, global competition analyses, net assessment, or forecasting to support decisionmakers on biotechnology advances.

Supporting the development of public-private partnerships with academia, industry, and other State and local government partners, including through the development or fostering of regionally focused innovation ecosystems.
Identifying biotechnology workforce and training gaps across the workforce of the Department.
Such other responsibilities as the Secretary considers appropriate.

subsection.text: This section shall terminate on September 30, 2030.

Not later than 30 days after the designation of the senior official pursuant to subsection (a), the Secretary shall provide to the congressional defense committees a briefing on the proposed scope of the charter for the office to be established pursuant to subsection (b), as well as implementation plans for preliminary activities the office will pursue during the proceeding one-year period.

section.header: Support for research and development of bioindustrial manufacturing processes
section.text: Section 215 of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023 (Public Law 117–263; 10 U.S.C. 4841 note) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsections (d) through (f) as subsections (e) through (g), respectively; and
by inserting after subsection (c) the following new subsection:

subsection.text: Funds authorized to be appropriated or otherwise made available to the Department of Defense for research, development, test, and evaluation may be used to carry out the activities described in subsection (c), including the design and construction activities described in subsection (c).
